ops.title: cc-steer-lab is being demoted/archived (2026-07-15) — all new work ships in the cc-steer repo as production-quality code; lab artifacts are read-only archive inputs
ops.body:
User directive (2026-07-15, during the data-quality pivot): "we're also in the process of
demoting and archiving the lab, do everything in-repo from now on, writing production-quality
code." Reinforces an earlier prompt from a parallel session: "I thought the entire point was
to get rid of the lab?"

**Why:** the lab (/Users/yasyf/Code/cc-steer-lab) was disposable scaffolding on the way to a
shippable system. Splitting logic across lab + package caused real drift: two divergent
`record_cell` variants, a freeze path that counted 27 empty-context rows and shrugged, and a
venv that re-breaks on `uv sync`. The retrain production loop already moved into the package
(cc-steer 8e72b62).

**How to apply:** new machinery — eval freeze/validity gates, migrations, experiment harness,
metrics, training loops — lands in /Users/yasyf/Code/cc-steer with tests, a clean public API,
and CI green. Never add code to cc-steer-lab. Treat lab contents (frozen eval-v2, results/,
adapters/, journals) as read-only historical artifacts to read from or migrate out. See
[[cc-steer-rename-decisions]], [[experiment-at-home-shared-infra]].
